sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: iShares ESG Aware MSCI USA ETF (NASDAQ: ESGU) - Share Price
feed.summary: The iShares ESG Aware MSCI USA ETF (ESGU) is currently trading at $140.39, representing a 1.70% decrease relative to its opening price today and a 1.89% decline over the past seven days. The article provides detailed share price performance over the current year, last year, and financial years, alongside a historical daily movement table stretching from March 2026 back to February 2026.

feed.title: Entera Bio Announces Full Year 2025 Financial Results and Provides Business Updates
feed.summary: Entera Bio reported its full-year 2025 financial results and provided updates on its pipeline, including significant progress with EB613 for osteoporosis, EB612 for hypoparathyroidism, and EB618 for metabolic syndromes. The company submitted a streamlined Phase 3 protocol for EB613, secured FDA alignment on BMD as a primary endpoint, and expanded its collaboration with OPKO Health for EB612. Financially, Entera Bio ended 2025 with $14.9 million in cash and cash equivalents, experiencing a net loss of $11.4 million.

feed.title: Fastenal posts Q1 webcast timing, presentation due 6 a.m. CT
feed.summary: Fastenal Company (Nasdaq:FAST) has announced that its conference call to discuss 2026 first-quarter results and current operations will be held on Monday, April 13, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. central time. The presentation, providing supplemental information, will be available at 6:00 a.m. central time on the same day. An online archive of the webcast will be accessible shortly after the call and will remain available until June 1, 2026.

feed.title: Daniel O'Day sells shares at Gilead Sciences (NASDAQ: GILD)
feed.summary: Daniel O'Day, a reporting person for Gilead Sciences (NASDAQ: GILD), filed a Form 144 notifying the SEC of proposed sales of Common shares. The filing lists examples of dispositions, including sales of 115,640 shares for $17,346,000 on February 5, 2026, and $15,611,400 on January 23, 2026. This Form 144 is a routine regulatory disclosure and does not confirm completed sales, which would be shown in subsequent SEC filings.

feed.title: Darden Restaurants (NYSE: DRI) grows sales, reshapes brands and boosts EPS
feed.summary: Darden Restaurants reported increased sales and mixed earnings in its latest quarter, with year-to-date sales growing 7.8% to $9.49 billion, driven by same-restaurant growth, new units, and the Chuy’s acquisition. The company is actively reshaping its brand portfolio by selling Olive Garden Canada and planning to close or convert 14 Bahama Breeze locations, while forecasting overall sales growth of 9.5% and 70 new restaurant openings for fiscal year 2026. Despite a slight dip in quarterly net earnings, year-to-date EPS from continuing operations rose to $6.91, supported by solid operating income and strong cash flows.

feed.title: 14 J.P. Morgan ETFs will switch exchange listings on April 16
feed.summary: J.P. Morgan Asset Management announced that 14 of its ETFs will switch exchange listings effective April 16, 2026. The funds, including JPHY, JEMA, and several BetaBuilders ETFs, will move between Cboe BZX, NYSE Arca, and Nasdaq exchanges. This change only affects the listing exchanges and not the funds' management or investment objectives.

feed.title: Larry Fink calls for Social Security reform, says investing a portion of funds could strengthen the program
feed.summary: BlackRock CEO Larry Fink has proposed reforms to Social Security, suggesting that investing a portion of its funds "carefully, broadly, and over decades" could strengthen the program and allow Americans to build wealth. He emphasized that this would not privatize Social Security but rather diversify its investments, similar to the federal Thrift Savings Plan. Fink highlighted that without reform, the Social Security trust fund faces insolvency by 2032, potentially leading to a 24% benefit cut for recipients.

feed.title: Camden Property Trust names new CEO amid C-suite shakeup
feed.summary: Ric Campo, the co-founder and long-time CEO of Camden Property Trust, has stepped down as part of the multifamily company's succession plan. He has been replaced by a new CEO. Campo's career with the development company spans 40 years, making him a significant figure in the industry.

feed.title: 5.00% Callable Notes due 2031 — Bank of America (NYSE: BAC)
feed.summary: Bank of America (NYSE: BAC) is offering 5.00% Fixed Rate Callable Notes due April 22, 2031. These senior, unsecured obligations will accrue interest semi-annually and are callable by BAC starting April 22, 2027. The notes are offered at 100.00% of principal with an underwriting discount, and a potential hedging-related charge may apply.

feed.title: HNR Acquisition Corp Announces Production Increases from Chemical Treatment Program
feed.summary: HNR Acquisition Corp (HNRA) reported successful oil production increases from a chemical stimulation program initiated in June 2024 in its Permian Basin properties. A pilot program on 24 wells yielded an additional 80+ barrels of oil per day (BOPD), and the company plans to expand this treatment to about 100 wells to eventually increase production by an estimated 250 BOPD by year-end 2024. This method is considered a safer, faster, and more economical way to enhance production, though results typically last around six months.

feed.title: Top Performing Leveraged/Inverse ETFs: 10/29/2023
feed.summary: This article identifies the top-performing leveraged and inverse ETFs from the week ending October 29, 2023. It explains that these funds can move rapidly due to leverage and lists the top ten, primarily inverse energy and high-beta/tech indices, along with one leveraged natural gas ETF. The article attributes the movements to factors like falling oil prices, high interest rates, disappointing earnings, and weather conditions.

feed.title: Apollo, BlackRock Deny Asking Kirkland To Abandon Optimum
feed.summary: Apollo, Ares, and BlackRock have denied accusations by Optimum Communications that they "bullied" Kirkland & Ellis LLP into withdrawing as Optimum's transaction counsel. Optimum claims this alleged pressure was retaliation for a collusion lawsuit it filed in New York federal court.

feed.title: Freedom Holding Corp.: Moody’s Assesses Freedom Bank Kazakhstan’s Financial Strength
feed.summary: Moody’s Ratings has assigned Freedom Bank Kazakhstan long-term deposit ratings of Ba3 with a stable outlook, reflecting its high growth rates, development of retail lending and digital products, and reduced reliance on investment income. The bank is integrating into the broader Freedom Holding Corp. ecosystem, which is actively expanding its geographical footprint across 21 countries and developing a unified digital platform called Freedom SuperApp. This expansion and ecosystem strategy aim to scale the business into new markets and offer a comprehensive suite of financial and digital services.

feed.title: Market Chatter: Occidental Petroleum CEO Hollub Preparing to Retire
feed.summary: Market rumors suggest that Occidental Petroleum CEO Vicki Hollub is making preparations to retire. This unconfirmed report from Seeking Alpha indicates a potential leadership transition at the major oil company.

feed.title: Halliburton hits two-year high as J.P. Morgan sees little Q1 earnings impact from Iran war
feed.summary: Halliburton's stock reached a two-year high after J.P. Morgan reiterated its Overweight rating and raised its price target to $40. Analysts anticipate that the company's Q1 earnings will remain consistent with estimates despite ongoing Middle East tensions. The stock was up 3.7% to $40.36.

feed.title: Ball Corp reports regional decline in recycled aluminium use in 2025
feed.summary: Ball Corporation reported a decline in the proportion of recycled aluminium used in its beverage packaging in North and Central America and South America in 2025, although the EMEA region saw an increase. Globally, 74% of the aluminium used was from recycled materials, unchanged from the previous year. The company aims for 85% recycled content by 2030 and collaborates with industry partners to improve recycling rates and design.

feed.title: Power Check: Entergy, Eaton and KLA
feed.summary: David Spika of Turtle Creek Wealth Advisors joined 'Power Lunch' to discuss his investing perspective on three specific stocks: Entergy, Eaton, and KLA. The segment provided Spika's insights on these companies for investors.

feed.title: Short Interest in Distribution Solutions Group, Inc. (NASDAQ:DSGR) Rises By 55.6%
feed.summary: Short interest in Distribution Solutions Group, Inc. (NASDAQ:DSGR) surged by 55.6% in March, reaching 473,119 shares, representing 4.4% of the stock. Despite this, a company director purchased 14,000 shares, increasing his holdings by over 42%, while recent quarterly results missed analyst expectations for both EPS and revenue. Analysts currently have a "Reduce" rating on the stock with an average price target of $35.00.

feed.title: Equinix launches AI platform to simplify control of distributed AI resources
feed.summary: Equinix has introduced the Distributed AI Hub, a new platform designed to centralize and simplify the control of distributed AI resources across data centers, edge locations, and clouds. The platform addresses the challenge of data sprawl in the AI era by offering unified connectivity, an AI-ready backbone, and tools like Fabric Intelligence for real-time awareness and automation of AI workloads. Additionally, Equinix is launching a global AI Solutions Lab and integrating security support from Palo Alto Networks to further facilitate AI migration and deployment for enterprises.

feed.title: Cincinnati Financial CEO gets major pay cut
feed.summary: Cincinnati Financial's CEO, Steve Spray, experienced a significant pay cut of over one-third in the past year, reducing his total compensation to $5.3 million. This pay reduction was a direct consequence of the company's performance metrics falling short, particularly its value creation ratio which lagged behind its peers. Spray's compensation in 2025 dropped by $3.2 million from the nearly $8.6 million he earned in 2024 when he was promoted to CEO.

feed.title: Hasbro Inc Stock: Navigating Toy and Game Market Dynamics for Long-Term Investors in 2026
feed.summary: This article analyzes Hasbro Inc for long-term investors, focusing on its business model, competitive strengths, and growth drivers in 2026. It highlights Hasbro's diversified portfolio through toys, digital gaming, and entertainment, driven by iconic brands and strategic IP maximization. The analysis also covers risks such as economic sensitivity and competition, while emphasizing Hasbro's North American market focus and dividend history.

feed.title: Why Gran Tierra is scaling up with Ecopetrol
feed.summary: Gran Tierra Energy views its new 49% stake in Colombia's Tisquirama block, secured with Ecopetrol, as a low-risk growth opportunity. The company plans to leverage its waterflood expertise and integrate the Tisquirama and San Roque fields with its existing Acordionero operations for enhanced recovery and reduced costs. Gran Tierra also highlighted the potential for natural gas in the block to support gas-to-power initiatives, aiming to lower operational costs and emissions.

feed.title: Investing in the future of Southwest Arkansas
feed.summary: ExxonMobil is investing in the future of Southwest Arkansas by supporting the region's emerging lithium industry and committing to community development. This includes investments in STEM education, workforce training, and various local programs, collaborating with organizations like LiTHIUMLEARNS, LiTHIUMWORKS, and Southern Arkansas University. The company aims for the growth of the lithium industry to help Southwest Arkansas communities thrive.

feed.title: Thermo Fisher Scientific Inc Stock (TMO) Moved Down by 3.31% on Mar 27: What Investors Need To Know
feed.summary: Thermo Fisher Scientific (TMO) stock dropped 3.31% due to several factors, including margin pressures, cautious 2026 guidance, and integration risks from its recent acquisition of Clario Holdings. The decline was also influenced by broader market sentiment, insider sales, and an institutional adjustment by Vanguard. Despite these pressures, analysts largely maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating for the stock.

feed.title: TJX Companies Inc Stock (TJX) Moved Down by 3.15% on Mar 27: Drivers Behind the Movement
feed.summary: TJX Companies Inc (TJX) stock declined by 3.15% on March 27, driven by broader retail sector weakness, macroeconomic concerns, and institutional selling despite strong financial performance. While the company reported increased sales and beat earnings, the industry faces subdued sentiment and valuation re-evaluation. Technical indicators show mixed signals, with a MACD buy signal but oversold conditions by Williams %R.

feed.title: Company encourages flexible support options as Dominion Energy stock consolidates amid mixed momentum
feed.summary: Dominion Energy is providing flexible support options for its customers while its stock, D, experiences consolidation. The stock shows short- to medium-term downside pressure but recovered 2.87% over the past week, indicating a potential for mild upside. Analysts forecast a high probability of price increase for the upcoming week with key consolidation expected between $60.09 support and $62.79 resistance.

feed.title: Block, Inc. Class A Revenue Breakdown – BSESOF:SQ3
feed.summary: This article provides a revenue breakdown for Block, Inc. Class A (SQ3) for the last year. It highlights that the company generated 20.60 billion EUR in revenue, with the Cash App segment contributing the largest portion at 13.13 billion EUR. The United States was the largest geographical contributor, accounting for 18.89 billion EUR of the revenue.

feed.title: Jim Cramer on Merck & Co.: “If We’re Really Going Into an Oil Shock-Induced Slowdown, I Think This One’s Likely an Outperformer””
feed.summary: Jim Cramer is bullish on Merck & Co. (NYSE: MRK), citing its strong performance driven by Keytruda and positive money flow. He views the stock as an outperformer, especially in an "oil shock-induced slowdown," and observes that recent pullbacks have been ideal entry points. Merck recently acquired a clinical-stage cancer drug company for $6.7 billion, a move that was well-received by the market.

feed.title: SDI says co-founder Richard Teets and Gabe Shaheen retiring from board
feed.summary: Steel Dynamics Inc. (SDI) announced that co-founder Richard "Dick" P. Teets, Jr. and Gabriel L. Shaheen will retire from its board of directors, effective May 6, 2026. CEO Mark Millett praised Teets for his foundational role in SDI's success and Shaheen for his invaluable financial and strategic contributions. Both retiring members expressed gratitude for their time and confidence in the company's future.

feed.title: Cruise Giants Face Financial Turbulence Amid Middle East Crisis
feed.summary: Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings Ltd. (NCLH) stock plummeted amid investor concerns over new travel restrictions stemming from Middle East conflicts. JPMorgan and BofA revised price targets downward due to lower EPS guidance, CEO transition, and geopolitical strains, with CFRA downgrading NCLH from Buy to Hold. The company faces significant financial vulnerabilities including missed Q4 revenue, high debt, and challenges in net yield growth and pricing pressures, exacerbated by oil price fluctuations and competitive pressures.

feed.title: Dyne Therapeutics Stock Slides After Rally Fizzles
feed.summary: Dyne Therapeutics (DYN) stock is experiencing a pullback as traders take profits after a recent sharp rally, creating a "sell-the-news" event. This decline is attributed to concerns over the company's significant cash burn and a larger-than-expected annual net loss, despite progress in its DELIVER and ACHIEVE programs towards a Q2 2026 BLA filing. Technical sell signals and rising trading volumes on down days are further reinforcing the stock's slide.

feed.title: Gas, diesel price 'sticker shock' top of mind for consumers, Tractor Supply Company CEO says
feed.summary: Tractor Supply Company CEO Hal Lawton states that the "sticker shock" of gas and diesel prices is a primary concern for consumers. He also discussed the impact of fuel costs during an appearance on 'Varney & Co.', linking it to President Donald Trump's White House meeting with farmers and ranchers. The video clip highlights the economic implications of current fuel prices.

feed.title: Vanguard disaggregates holdings; Westinghouse shows 0 shares after realignment (WAB)
feed.summary: Vanguard Group has filed an amendment (Schedule 13G/A) for Westinghouse Air Brake Technologies Corp (WAB), reporting 0 shares owned and a 0% stake. This change is due to an internal realignment effective January 12, 2026, where certain Vanguard subsidiaries will now report beneficial ownership separately. The filing clarifies that this disaggregation is for reporting purposes under SEC Release No. 34-39538 and does not represent an economic sale of shares.

feed.title: Essex Property Trust (NYSE:ESS) Sets New 52-Week Low - Time to Sell?
feed.summary: Essex Property Trust (ESS) recently hit a new 52-week low, trading at $239.24. Analysts largely hold a "Hold" rating for the stock, with a consensus target price of $282.00, despite the company slightly missing quarterly EPS estimates but raising its dividend.

feed.title: Dow's 734-Point Drop Led By Losses For Amazon.Com Inc., UnitedHealth Stocks
feed.summary: The Dow Jones Industrial Average dropped 734 points, primarily due to losses in Amazon.Com Inc. and UnitedHealth stocks. This significant decline indicates a negative day for the overall market, driven by underperformance from these major companies.

feed.title: Fortinet Stock Falls As Anthropic AI Leak Pressures Cybersecurity Stocks
feed.summary: Fortinet (NASDAQ: FTNT) stock fell after an alleged Anthropic AI leak raised concerns about AI's impact on cybersecurity, leading to sector-wide pressure. Although Anthropic clarified the incident was human error unrelated to AI tools, investors fear AI-native solutions could disrupt traditional security markets, putting valuation pressure on established companies like Fortinet.

feed.title: What Trump said about Caterpillar, other top agriculture brand costs
feed.summary: Former President Donald Trump announced that he wants major agriculture brands like John Deere, Case, and Caterpillar to reduce the costs of their tractors and equipment for farmers. Following his statement, Deere & Co shares dropped by 2%, and Case manufacturer CNH saw a 1% decline, while Caterpillar remained largely unchanged. Trump also outlined new loan guarantees for farmers and food suppliers through the U.S. Small Business Administration, updated renewable fuel standards, and stated he would seek further congressional relief for the agricultural sector.

feed.title: Raymond James Financial Has Lowered Expectations for BRP (TSE:DOO) Stock Price
feed.summary: Raymond James Financial has decreased its price target for BRP (TSE:DOO) from C$117 to C$115, suggesting a 24.7% upside. Despite this, several other analysts recently raised their targets, leading to a consensus "Moderate Buy" rating with an average price target of C$116.29. BRP shares were trading down 0.9% at C$92.20, with a market cap of C$6.74 billion and a P/E ratio of 19.87, and reported C$2.21 EPS on C$2.46 billion in revenue for the quarter.

feed.title: Donaldson (NYSE: DCI) president sells 20,600 shares after option exercise
feed.summary: Donaldson Co. president Guillermo Briseño completed an exercise-and-sell transaction involving 20,600 shares of common stock. On March 26, 2026, he exercised fully vested employee stock options across two tranches and subsequently sold all 20,600 shares at $84.97 per share. Following these transactions, Briseño directly holds 29,267 shares of Donaldson common stock.

feed.title: Kroger Investing In Ohio Stores
feed.summary: Kroger is investing $112 million in Ohio to build new stores and remodel existing ones. This expenditure aims to enhance the grocer's presence, support communities, and provide modern shopping experiences in central and northwest Ohio. The plan includes two new Kroger Marketplace locations opening this fall and renovations at 15 other stores.

feed.title: Internet Giants ETF Cuts Meta and Alphabet in Rebalance
feed.summary: The ALPS O’Shares Global Internet Giants ETF (OGIG) significantly reduced its exposure to Meta Platforms and Alphabet during its March rebalancing, capping both positions at 6%. This strategic shift aims to de-emphasize mega-cap stocks and capitalize on more attractive valuations in enterprise software infrastructure and undervalued internet platforms, aligning with Morningstar's 2026 Global Outlook. The fund added companies like Synopsys and Constellation Software while removing nine other holdings, and increased its weight in Oracle and Tencent, among others.

feed.title: Raymond James: Credit Union Program Partnership With Addition Financial
feed.summary: Raymond James Financial's Financial Institutions Division has partnered with Addition Financial Credit Union to support its investment program, Addition Financial Wealth Management. This new alignment will provide comprehensive wealth management services to Addition Financial's clients through Raymond James Financial Services. The partnership, led by financial advisor Jay Williams, aims to expand investment capabilities and offer digital tools to better serve credit union members with their financial planning needs.

feed.title: 51Talk full year 2025 financial and operational results
feed.summary: 51Talk Online Education Group (NYSE:COE) announced strong financial results for the full year ended December 31, 2025, with net revenues surging by 88.6% to $95.6 million, driven by its international business expansion. Active students increased by 79.3%, reaching approximately 170.3 thousand, reflecting robust demand in Southeast Asia and other emerging markets. While the company achieved a healthy operating cash inflow, increased sales and marketing expenses and investments in its AI platform led to widened operating and net losses for the year.

feed.title: Bank of Montreal (BERZ) issues $5.61M 4.25% notes due 2029
feed.summary: Bank of Montreal has issued $5.61 million in Series K Senior Medium-Term Notes, bearing a 4.25% interest rate and maturing on March 30, 2029. Interest will be paid semi-annually, with the bank retaining the option to redeem the notes early starting March 30, 2027. These notes are unsecured, not exchange-listed, and are subject to Canadian bail-in provisions under the CDIC Act.

feed.title: Verizon Communications Inc. Stock: Steady Growth Amid Broadband Expansion and Solid Dividend Yield
feed.summary: Verizon Communications Inc. has experienced significant stock growth in 2026 due to strong earnings, broadband expansion, and improved guidance. The company’s focus on integrated wireless and broadband services, coupled with a solid 5.6% dividend yield, makes it an attractive option for North American investors seeking defensive telecom exposure. While it faces risks like debt levels and competitive discounting, Verizon's strategic initiatives and consistent performance underscore its potential for moderate annualized returns.

feed.title: KBW Maintains Aflac Inc(AFL.US) With Hold Rating, Cuts Target Price to $113
feed.summary: KBW analyst Ryan Krueger has maintained a "Hold" rating on Aflac Inc (AFL.US) but adjusted the target price down from $115 to $113. Krueger has a 62.9% success rate and a 9.2% average return over the past year. This report highlights key analyst metrics provided by TipRanks, an independent third party.

feed.title: Kroger to reportedly open two stores, invest $112 million in Ohio
feed.summary: Kroger plans to invest $112 million in Ohio to open two new stores and renovate existing ones, as part of a larger strategy to accelerate capital investment and expand its competitive position. The investment includes new Kroger Marketplace locations and enhanced shopping experiences in central and northwest Ohio, with two new stores costing approximately $30 million each. This expansion aligns with Kroger's aim to become a national retailer and increase new store builds by 30% in 2026.

feed.title: Fastenal stock consolidates as team gears up for Martinsville race
feed.summary: Fastenal (FAST) stock is consolidating near recent highs, trading between $44.70 and $45.30, exhibiting a balance of bullish and bearish momentum. The company announced its sponsorship for Ryan Preece and the No. 60 Body Guard Ford team at Martinsville. Technical indicators suggest short-to-medium-term selling pressure, but the long-term trend remains strong, with a high probability of price increase in the coming week.

feed.title: Vanguard disaggregates holdings; Waters Corp shows 0% stake (WAT)
feed.summary: Vanguard Group has filed an Amendment No. 16 to Schedule 13G/A, reporting 0% beneficial ownership and 0 shares in Waters Corp (WAT). This change is attributed to an internal realignment on January 12, 2026, where certain Vanguard subsidiaries will now report their holdings separately, referencing SEC Release No. 34-39538. The filing indicates a disaggregation of reporting rather than a sale of shares.

feed.title: Multimillion-dollar paycheck: Here's how much Norwegian's new CEO could make
feed.summary: Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings' new CEO, John Chidsey, is set to receive a multimillion-dollar compensation package. His pay is tied to the company's long-term performance, aligning with ongoing governance changes at the Miami-based cruise operator.
